meta.title: DBX-CEA-1: System Prompts, RAG, Memory and MCP Tools

questions.stem: An agent must always answer in JSON with fields `answer` and `citations`. Where should that requirement be placed for the MOST consistent behavior?

options.text: In the system prompt or equivalent top-level instructions
Only in the final user message
Inside retrieved documents
Inside a warehouse policy comment

questions.explanation: Persistent formatting and behavioral constraints belong in the system-level instructions because they govern every turn. Retrieved documents and user messages are less reliable for durable control.
questions.reference: Databricks Context Engineer certification page; prompt design guidance

questions.stem: A prompt engineer keeps adding long persona text that does not affect task quality but consumes tokens. What is the BEST improvement?

options.text: Keep only instructions that materially change agent behavior and remove decorative prompt text
Add more adjectives so the agent stays in character
Move the persona text into every retrieved chunk
Replace task instructions with chain-of-thought requirements

questions.explanation: Context engineering emphasizes useful signal in the context window. Extra decorative persona text consumes tokens without helping task completion.
questions.reference: Databricks certification page; context window management guidance

questions.stem: A team wants a managed semantic search index that stays synchronized with a Delta table automatically. Which Databricks option BEST fits?

options.text: A delta-sync Vector Search index
A notebook-scoped pandas DataFrame
A SQL warehouse result cache
A model registry alias

questions.explanation: Delta-sync indexes keep a vector search index synchronized with a source Delta table. The other options are not managed semantic retrieval indexes.
questions.reference: Databricks Vector Search documentation

questions.stem: Search quality drops because each chunk contains many unrelated topics from long documents. Which change is MOST likely to help?

options.text: Use a chunking strategy that preserves local semantic coherence
Increase warehouse auto-stop timeout
Disable embeddings and use only row-level security
Store every document as one very large chunk

questions.explanation: Better chunking improves retrieval because embeddings then represent one coherent topic instead of several unrelated ones. Very large chunks usually hurt recall and precision.
questions.reference: Databricks RAG and vector search guidance

questions.stem: A retrieval system needs to balance semantic similarity with exact keyword matching for product codes. Select TWO approaches that BEST help. (Select TWO)

options.text: Use hybrid retrieval that combines dense and sparse signals
Use re-ranking to improve the final ordering of retrieved results
Remove all metadata fields from the index
Increase the model temperature during generation

questions.explanation: Hybrid retrieval helps with exact tokens like product IDs while re-ranking improves final relevance ordering. Metadata removal and generation temperature do not solve retrieval ranking.
questions.reference: Databricks AI Search / Vector Search guidance; retrieval best practices

questions.stem: A support agent must remember important user preferences across separate sessions days apart. Which memory design is MOST appropriate?

options.text: Persist long-term memory outside the immediate chat window and retrieve it when relevant
Keep all historical turns in the active prompt forever
Rely only on the model's pretraining
Use a larger warehouse for every query

questions.explanation: Cross-session memory requires external persistence and selective retrieval. Keeping everything in-context forever is expensive and eventually impossible because of token limits.
questions.reference: Databricks agent systems guidance; memory architecture concepts

questions.stem: Conversation history is causing prompt bloat, but the agent still needs prior decisions. Which technique BEST addresses this?

options.text: Summarize older turns into a compact state representation
Duplicate the entire chat transcript in the system prompt
Delete all history after every message
Move the history into column comments

questions.explanation: Summarization preserves key facts while reducing token usage. Duplicating the transcript worsens prompt bloat, and deleting everything loses useful context.
questions.reference: Context window management and memory summarization guidance

questions.stem: What is the PRIMARY value of exposing a well-defined tool schema to an agent?

options.text: It lets the model understand the tool's inputs and outputs so it can call the tool more reliably
It guarantees the model will never hallucinate
It replaces authentication and governance controls
It eliminates the need for prompt instructions

questions.explanation: A clear tool schema improves reliable tool invocation by making inputs and outputs explicit. It does not replace governance or eliminate all model errors.
questions.reference: Model Context Protocol concepts; Databricks agent tooling guidance

questions.stem: A governed analytics agent should query sensitive tables only through approved interfaces. Which design BEST supports this goal?

options.text: Expose curated Unity Catalog functions or governed tools instead of giving the model unrestricted raw table access
Let the model generate arbitrary SQL against every catalog
Embed secrets in the prompt
Bypass Unity Catalog and read cloud storage directly

questions.explanation: Governed tools and functions constrain what the agent can do and allow policy enforcement. Arbitrary access and embedded secrets create unnecessary risk.
questions.reference: Unity Catalog functions; Databricks governance for AI applications

questions.stem: A team is integrating external tools into an agent. Select TWO good practices. (Select TWO)

options.text: Validate tool inputs against the declared schema
Return clear error information so the agent can recover or ask for clarification
Hide tool side effects from logs to save storage
Make every tool accept one unstructured free-text string only

questions.explanation: Input validation and clear error handling improve reliability and recovery. Opaque free-text tool signatures and missing observability make tool use harder to govern.
questions.reference: Tool design and MCP-style integration best practices

questions.stem: Which statement BEST describes token budgeting in a production agent?

options.text: You must account for instructions, retrieved context, tool traces, and conversation history within the model limit
Only user messages count toward context length
Tokens matter only for cost, not model limits
Result caching eliminates token limits

questions.explanation: All prompt components consume context-window capacity, so token budgeting is a core context-engineering task. Caches do not remove model limits.
questions.reference: Databricks context engineering page; LLM context window basics

questions.stem: An agent frequently overflows its context window after retrieving too many chunks. Which mitigation is MOST targeted?

options.text: Reduce and prioritize retrieved context, then summarize or prune lower-value history
Increase response temperature
Store chunks in a second notebook
Disable citations

questions.explanation: Overflow is best addressed by better retrieval selection and prompt compaction. Temperature and citations do not meaningfully reduce context size.
questions.reference: RAG and context compaction guidance

questions.stem: A team wants to compare two prompt-and-retrieval configurations against a fixed evaluation set. Which approach is BEST?

options.text: Run both configurations against the same benchmark data and compare quality metrics side by side
Ask one engineer which output feels better after reading two examples
Change the prompt in production for all users immediately
Evaluate only latency because correctness is subjective

questions.explanation: Controlled side-by-side evaluation on a shared dataset is the right way to compare configurations. Anecdotal feedback and production-only changes are weaker evidence.
questions.reference: MLflow evaluation and Databricks quality testing guidance

questions.stem: A governance review is focused on preventing sensitive information from entering prompts. Select TWO controls that BEST align with this goal. (Select TWO)

options.text: Tag and classify sensitive sources so retrieval or tool access can be restricted
Apply policies that filter or mask data before it reaches agent context
Increase top-k retrieval so relevant records are less likely to be missed
Require every answer to be cheerful and concise

questions.explanation: Sensitive-data protection depends on classification and enforcement before context assembly. Retrieval breadth and answer tone do not provide privacy controls.
questions.reference: Unity Catalog governance features; responsible AI guidance

questions.stem: A team measures answer quality only after generation and ignores retrieval diagnostics. What key risk does this create?

options.text: They may miss that poor answers are caused by bad context assembly rather than model generation alone
They will automatically lower storage costs
They guarantee better tool routing
They remove the need for offline evaluation

questions.explanation: Context engineering requires observing retrieval and assembly quality, not just final answers. Otherwise teams cannot tell whether failures come from missing or poor context.
questions.reference: Databricks context engineering and observability concepts
